notes: Sentence-final 'dong' softens a command/request — 'Cepetan' ('hurry up') without dong is curt; with dong it's affectionate-impatient (the way you'd say it to a partner, not a stranger). English 'please' lands the soft-command register. VERIFY WITH GIRLFRIEND: 'come on' might capture the affectionate-impatient layer better in some contexts.
